title: psoralea arborescens
intro: Psoralea arborescens, also known as wolf's bane or Indian turnip, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible roots. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Psoralea arborescens include its potential to improve skin health, reduce scarring, and promote hair growth. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
